categories.name: Identity & Auth
categories.description: Who is logging in, how they prove it, and the sessions that keep them logged in. The gatekeeper for everything downstream.
categories.tech_notes: Own our own auth. NestJS module in culinary-ops. Postgres (Prisma) for AuthIdentity/Session/OtpCode/OAuthLink. Twilio Verify for SMS. Resend for email. Google + Facebook OAuth with our own app registrations. JWT access tokens (15 min) + opaque refresh tokens (30 days) in httpOnly cookies. argon2id for password hashing.

modules.name: Auth Foundation
modules.description: Schemas, middleware, and primitives every other auth module depends on.

components.name: AuthIdentity model
components.description: Primary auth row. One per phone or email. Holds password hash, verification timestamps, last login.

tech.notes: Fields: id, email?, phone?, password_hash?, email_verified_at?, phone_verified_at?, created_at, last_login_at

components.name: Session model
components.description: Refresh-token sessions. One row per active login.

tech.notes: Fields: id, identity_id, refresh_token_hash, user_agent, ip_address, expires_at, revoked_at?

components.name: OtpCode model
components.description: One-time codes for SMS or email OTP. Short TTL, hashed values, attempt counter.

tech.notes: Fields: id, identity_id?, destination, channel (sms|email), code_hash, attempts, expires_at, consumed_at?

components.name: OAuthLink model
components.description: Links third-party identity providers (Google, Facebook) to an AuthIdentity.

tech.notes: Fields: id, identity_id, provider, provider_id, email, linked_at. @@unique([provider, provider_id])

components.name: JWT signer + verifier
components.description: Sign short-lived access tokens, verify on every protected request. Refresh flow.

tech.notes: jsonwebtoken or jose. 15-minute access token, 30-day refresh. Our own signing secret from env.

components.name: AuthGuard decorator
components.description: Decorator to protect API routes. Extracts identity from JWT, attaches to request context.

tech.notes: @UseGuards(AuthGuard) on protected controllers.

components.name: Rate limiter
components.description: Per-IP and per-identity rate limits on login, signup, OTP send, password reset.

tech.notes: @nestjs/throttler or a Redis-backed custom limiter.

modules.name: Phone OTP Login
modules.description: Passwordless login with a 6-digit SMS code. The default sign-in method for most customers.

components.name: Send OTP endpoint
components.description: POST /api/auth/phone/start — normalizes phone, looks up or creates AuthIdentity, triggers Twilio Verify send, returns otp_id.

tech.notes: Twilio Verify SID in env. Uses their service — we don't manage code generation or storage ourselves.

components.name: Verify OTP endpoint
components.description: POST /api/auth/phone/verify — checks code with Twilio, marks phone verified, creates session, returns JWT + refresh token.

components.name: Phone input + country code

tech.notes: libphonenumber-js for validation and formatting.

components.name: 6-digit code entry
components.description: Auto-advancing 6-box code entry with paste support and resend link.

tech.notes: argon2id with sensible work factors. Verification email sent via Resend.

components.name: Email verification link

components.name: POST /api/auth/login

tech.notes: Returns 401 with reason='must_reset_password' when legacy HMP user has no local hash.

modules.name: Session Management
modules.description: Active-session lifecycle, refresh token rotation, logout, multi-device support.

components.name: POST /api/auth/refresh
components.description: Trade opaque refresh token for new access token. Rotate refresh token on every use.

tech.notes: Refresh stored as httpOnly cookie. Rotation detects replay — if old token used after rotation, revoke entire session chain.

components.name: Logout current session
components.description: Revoke the current refresh token, clear cookie.

components.name: Logout all devices
components.description: Revoke every session for this identity. Used after password change or suspected compromise.

components.name: List + revoke sessions UI
components.description: Account page showing all active sessions with user agent, IP, last active. Per-session revoke button.

modules.name: Account Recovery
modules.description: Forgot password, password reset, lost-phone recovery.

components.name: POST /api/auth/forgot-password
components.description: Accept email, send reset link via Resend. SKIPPED - commerce auth is 100% passwordless, no forgot-password flow ever.
components.notes: 2026-04-09 (Conner): SKIPPED by architectural decision. BetterDay D2C customer auth is 100% passwordless - magic link + phone OTP + Apple/Google OAuth only. There are no passwords, so there is nothing to reset. If a customer loses access to their email, they use phone OTP as an alternate recovery path. If they lose both, CS support handles account recovery manually. Locked decision - see memory: project_betterday_passwordless_auth.

components.name: POST /api/auth/reset-password
components.description: Accept token + new password, verify, update hash, revoke all existing sessions. SKIPPED - commerce auth is 100% passwordless, no password to reset.
components.notes: 2026-04-09 (Conner): SKIPPED alongside auth.recovery.forgot.request. No password_hash field exists on the Customer table in the commerce schema, so there's nothing for this endpoint to reset. Locked decision - see memory: project_betterday_passwordless_auth.

components.name: Customer Prisma model
components.description: Core customer identity + profile + auth state, all on one table. See notes for architectural divergence from the original AuthIdentity-linked design.

tech.database: Prisma + Postgres (Neon, betterday-commerce project - SEPARATE from culinary-ops DB)
tech.notes: Lives in backend/prisma/commerce/schema.prisma in a dedicated Neon project (spring-fire-44871408) under Conner's BetterDay Food Co org. Fields include display_id, email (unique), phone, first_name, last_name, birthday, member_since, status enum, source enum, apple_id_sub, google_id_sub, helcim_customer_id, email_verified + email_verified_at, phone_verified + phone_verified_at, sms_opt_in, email_opt_in, allergens[], diet_tags[], disliked_meals[], favorite_meals[], points_balance, internal_notes, tags[], flagged, timestamps.

components.notes: 2026-04-09 (Conner): Shipped in Migration 1 (20260408234132_init) on culinary-ops conner/universal-brand-folder. ARCHITECTURAL DIVERGENCE from this scope board's AuthIdentity-linked design: auth fields live DIRECTLY on Customer (no separate AuthIdentity table), because commerce auth is 100% passwordless. No password_hash field by design - magic link + phone OTP + Apple/Google OAuth only. See memory: project_betterday_passwordless_auth. The auth.foundation.schema.* nodes in this scope board still describe the old AuthIdentity design and need to be reconciled in a dedicated future pass.

components.name: Lifecycle stage enum
components.description: prospect | active | paused | churned. Drives re-engagement campaigns and admin filters.

tech.notes: Enum in Prisma schema. Updated by subscription state transitions.

components.name: CustomerAddress Prisma model
components.description: Delivery or pickup address. Recipient contact fields are distinct from the customer's own profile - supports gift addresses where Jose stores Mom's address with Mom's contact details.

tech.notes: Fields: id, customer_id, label, type (AddressType enum), recipient_first_name, recipient_last_name, recipient_email, recipient_phone (all required), company, street, street2, city, state, zip, delivery_instructions, is_default, timestamps. Hard delete, not soft delete - orders keep their shipping_address_id via onDelete SetNull so the order row survives.

components.notes: 2026-04-09 (Conner): Shipped in Migration 1 and refined in Migration 2 (20260409033641_add_points_coupons_address_contact) which split recipient_name into first/last/email/phone to match the subscriber-hub-2.0 Edit Address form. DIVERGENCE from the original spec: no is_default_delivery + is_default_billing split - just one is_default per address, scoped by type via composite index. No archived_at soft delete - onDelete SetNull on the FK from CustomerOrder preserves order history.

components.name: CustomerPreference model
components.description: Dietary restrictions, allergens, diet tags, disliked meals, favorite meals. Implemented as array columns on Customer rather than a separate table - see notes.

tech.notes: Implemented as String[] columns on Customer: allergens, diet_tags, disliked_meals, favorite_meals. No separate CustomerPreference table. This is a v1 simplification - if we ever need weighted preferences or preference history, we'd promote to a real table.

components.notes: 2026-04-09 (Conner): Shipped as array fields on Customer in Migration 1. Design choice: simpler than a separate table for the current flat-list preference model. The PATCH /api/commerce/customers/me/preferences endpoint REPLACES the full array on each write (not merge). If future work needs richer preference semantics (weights, history, scoped-to-week), we'd promote to a dedicated CustomerPreference table - not yet needed.

modules.description: Read/write endpoints for profile data, called from the account hub.

components.name: GET /api/commerce/customers/me
components.description: Returns current customer + addresses + payment_methods + subscription. Admin-only fields (internal_notes, tags, flagged, last_contacted_at) stripped before response.

tech.notes: URL path diverges from the original GET /api/account/me spec because all commerce endpoints are namespaced under /api/commerce/ to keep them cleanly separated from Gurleen's culinary-side /api/ routes.

components.notes: 2026-04-09 (Conner): Shipped as part of commerce-customers NestJS module in backend/src/modules/commerce-customers/ on culinary-ops conner/universal-brand-folder. Uses a dev-stub @CurrentCustomer() decorator that reads x-dev-customer-id header - MUST be replaced with a real CustomerAuthGuard before production.

components.name: PATCH /api/commerce/customers/me
components.description: Update first_name, last_name, phone, birthday. Also ships PATCH me/preferences (allergens, diet_tags, disliked_meals, favorite_meals) and PATCH me/notifications (sms_opt_in, email_opt_in) as sibling endpoints.

tech.notes: Uses PATCH semantics (omitted fields left unchanged) instead of PUT. Validated via class-validator DTOs. Email and phone changes are NOT in this endpoint - those require CustomerAuthToken round-trips and belong in a future commerce-auth module.

components.notes: 2026-04-09 (Conner): Shipped in commerce-customers module. URL path diverges from original PUT /api/account/profile - namespaced under /api/commerce/customers/ and uses PATCH verb.

components.name: Address CRUD
components.description: Full address management: list, create, update, delete, set-as-default. Default invariant enforced atomically via transaction (at most one is_default per customer per type).

tech.notes: Each mutation asserts the address belongs to the current customer via assertAddressOwnedBy() before proceeding - returns 403 if another customer's UUID is probed. setDefaultAddress uses $transaction to unset any existing default of the same type before setting the new one.

components.notes: 2026-04-09 (Conner): Shipped 5 endpoints (not 3 as spec'd) - added list + set-default as separate routes. Uses PATCH instead of PUT for updates, namespaced under /api/commerce/customers/.

components.description: Filter by dietary tag or allergen.
components.notes: 2026-04-09 (Conner): Data layer to back this filter landed on the culinary-ops side. MealRecipe.diet_plan_id is now a NOT NULL FK to SystemTag where type='diets' (two rows: Omnivore fc0a70f3-644b-4248-b9c1-65882cc503de and Plant-Based 9c68ba40-f59d-40a8-8210-bdc1f3cd3973). 159 meals classified on the conner-local-dev Neon branch: 88 Omnivore + 71 Plant-Based, 0 nulls. 47 meat->plant variant pairings established one-directionally via linked_meal_id. The customer-facing filter endpoint itself still needs building, but the query it runs is now a single-column WHERE instead of string matching on dietary_tags arrays. See culinary-ops PR #10 (meal-edit-menu-cats-refactor) and ADR 2026-04-08-mandatory-diet-plan-on-dishes.md for the full design.

components.notes: 2026-04-09 (Conner): The SystemTag table (type=menu-cats) is now the single source of truth for valid category values across admin and customer-facing surfaces. Current tags: Entree, Breakfast, Snacks, Sandwich & Wraps, Protein Pack. Admin meal list page and meal edit page both consume the same api.getTags() call filtered to type='menu-cats', so any new tab added in /settings/tags propagates to both the admin dropdown and (eventually) the customer menu tabs. Bulk backfill applied to the 142 priced Meat/Vegan meals on the conner-local-dev Neon branch -> all now category=Entree. Final category distribution: Entree 142, Breakfast 10, Snacks 7. When this component is ready to build, consume from menu-cats, not a hardcoded list. Customer website will eventually need a public unauthenticated version of /api/tags (currently JWT-guarded) to read this without admin auth.

modules.name: Subscription Engine
modules.description: The cron jobs and logic that make a subscription tick week over week.

components.name: Friday morning rollover job
components.description: For every non-cancelled subscription, create next week's WeeklyCartRecord. Copy previous week's items, run auto-swap, set status=scheduled (or paused if account is paused).

components.name: Idempotent rollover
components.description: Re-running should be a no-op; check for existing record for delivery_week before inserting.

components.name: Auto-swap on rollover
components.description: If a meal from last week isn't on next week's menu, pick a replacement in the same category matching dietary preferences.

tech.notes: Uses CustomerPreference to rank candidates.

components.name: Swap fallback
components.description: If no same-category match exists, pick the closest match and flag the record for customer review.

components.name: Swap notification
components.description: Optional email: 'We swapped X for Y this week - tap to review'.

components.name: Thursday cutoff job
components.description: Every Thursday 23:59 (or configured time), flip every 'scheduled' WeeklyCartRecord to 'confirmed', charge the card, generate the production order.

components.name: Charge at cutoff
components.description: Create Stripe charge via subscription + update invoice. On success mark confirmed. On failure mark payment_failed and notify.

components.name: Create production Order
components.description: Cutoff creates an immutable Order record for production (one per weekly record that flipped to confirmed).

components.name: Cart report SQL
components.description: SELECT meal_id, SUM(qty) FROM weekly_cart_items JOIN weekly_cart_records WHERE delivery_week=X AND delivery_status IN ('scheduled','confirmed') GROUP BY meal_id.

tech.notes: The production kitchen reads this to know what to make.

tech.notes: Validated against CouperMirror.orders_completed_count live at redemption.

components.name: Subscription restriction
components.description: subscription_only | non_subscription_only | any.

components.name: Payment method restriction
components.description: Restrict to specific payment methods (Apple Pay only, etc.).
components.notes: 2026-04-09 (Conner): Explicitly disabled in the coupon feature picker. Per the coupon stacking policy, coupons deliberately stack on subscription discounts to incentivize first-time subscription sign-ups — payment-method restriction is over-engineering for the current scope and not part of Migration 5 (coupon_power_up). Keep as skipped until there's a clear business case.

components.name: CouponAttachedProduct model
components.description: Products auto-added to cart when coupon applies (BOGO, free gift).

components.name: CouponRedemption model
components.description: Fields: id, coupon_id, customer_id, order_id, redeemed_at, amount_cents, was_first_order, order_count_at_redemption. Unique constraint on (coupon_id, order_id).
components.notes: 2026-04-09 (Conner): Migration 5 did not create a separate CouponRedemption table. Instead, redemption data lives on the existing CustomerCoupon model: amount_discounted (savings snapshot), redeemed_order_id (FK to CustomerOrder), redeemed_at (timestamp), plus the new UTM-style attribution fields source_channel, source_campaign, source_content, referrer_url. Different architecture than originally planned but covers the same reporting needs. See backend/prisma/commerce/migrations/20260409060452_coupon_power_up/ in the culinary-ops repo.

components.name: CouponTier model
components.description: New child table for tiered spend discounts. One Coupon can have multiple tiers with min_spend thresholds, each with its own discount type, value, and optional free delivery flag. Evaluated highest-matching-tier-first at checkout. Built in Migration 5 (coupon_power_up).

components.name: Source channel enum on CustomerCoupon
components.description: CouponSourceChannel enum (email, sms, landing_page, in_app, in_cart, direct, referral, partner, flyer, influencer, unknown). Populated at redemption time. Enables per-channel reporting.

components.name: Source campaign on CustomerCoupon
components.description: Free-text utm_campaign equivalent. Slices channel-level reporting into individual campaigns (e.g. spring_launch_2026).

components.name: Source content on CustomerCoupon
components.description: Free-text utm_content equivalent. Tracks which specific creative, influencer, or ad variant drove a redemption.

components.name: Referrer URL on CustomerCoupon
components.description: HTTP referrer at redemption time (web only). Captures organic discovery paths that were not tagged with UTM params.

components.name: Referrer customer FK on Coupon
components.description: referrer_customer_id field on Coupon linking a coupon to the customer who generated or sent it. On delete: SET NULL. Enables referral programs where redeeming a friend's code credits both parties.

components.name: Referrer credit amount on Coupon
components.description: referrer_credit Decimal field. The credit the referring customer receives when someone else redeems this coupon.

components.name: Referrer credit type on Coupon
components.description: referrer_credit_type field (reuses CouponType enum). Whether the referrer gets a percentage, fixed dollar amount, free item, or free delivery credit.

components.name: Customer tag include/exclude on Coupon
components.description: target_customer_tags and exclude_customer_tags String[] fields. Restricts coupon to, or excludes, customers with specific admin-assigned tags like VIP or frequent-edits.

components.name: Lifetime spend range on Coupon
components.description: min_lifetime_spend and max_lifetime_spend Decimal fields. Restricts coupon to customers within an LTV band. Enables VIP perks at the high end and win-back offers at the low end.

components.name: Member-since range on Coupon
components.description: min_member_since_days and max_member_since_days Int fields. Targets customer cohorts by account age (e.g. customers who signed up in the last 30 days).

components.name: Customer status targeting on Coupon
components.description: target_customer_statuses CustomerStatus[] field. Restricts by active, paused_indefinite, cancelled, unclaimed states. Enables win-back targeting specifically at cancelled accounts.

modules.name: Deal of the Week
modules.description: Scheduled weekly featured discounts on specific meals. Thin wrapper over the coupon engine — most fields are preset (purpose=deal_of_the_week, auto_apply=true, show_as_clippable=true), admin only picks the dish(es), delivery week, and discount amount. See conner/dotw-scheduler-mockup.html in culinary-ops for the scheduler UI prototype.
modules.tech_notes: Implemented as a subset of the Coupon table using the new purpose enum and delivery_week_sunday column added in Migration 5. No separate DOTW table. Validation rule: at checkout, if coupon.purpose == deal_of_the_week then order.delivery_week_sunday must match coupon.delivery_week_sunday. See project_dotw_preorder_rules memory for the three governing rules (week binding, pre-order visibility filter, cart price ceiling).

components.name: CouponPurpose.deal_of_the_week
components.description: The deal_of_the_week value in the CouponPurpose enum. Distinguishes DOTW coupons from manual, auto_applied, welcome, winback, birthday, abandoned_cart, and referral coupons. Added in Migration 5.

components.name: delivery_week_sunday column on Coupon
components.description: Nullable Date column. When set, the coupon only applies to orders whose delivery week matches this Sunday. Enables pre-scheduling DOTWs months ahead and avoiding the pre-order leak edge case where a current-week DOTW would silently apply to future-week orders.

components.name: Delivery week matching rule
components.description: Backend validator check: if coupon.purpose == deal_of_the_week, require order.delivery_week_sunday == coupon.delivery_week_sunday, else reject with a WRONG_DELIVERY_WEEK error. Prevents current-week DOTWs from applying to future-week pre-orders.
components.notes: 2026-04-09 (Conner): Phase 1 next-up target. The coupon schema (Migration 5) is complete and this leaf is unblocked. See project-scope/NEXT_UP.md for the detailed Phase 1 plan and the order these four leaves should be built in.

components.description: Self-contained HTML prototype at conner/dotw-scheduler-mockup.html in the culinary-ops repo. 8-week rolling calendar view, editable presets panel, modal meal picker, and coupon JSON export. Demonstrates the locked-preset approach (purpose + auto_apply + show_as_clippable + category + tags all pre-filled, admin only picks dish + discount + display message).

components.name: Real DOTW scheduler in admin UI

components.name: DOTW CRUD endpoints

Thin wrapper over the generic Coupon CRUD but with locked presets (purpose=deal_of_the_week, auto_apply=true, show_as_clippable=true, category=seasonal by default). Admin only supplies dish, discount, and delivery_week_sunday.

components.name: Menu read bridge
components.description: Direct Prisma queries against MealRecipe/MealComponent/PortionSpec.
components.notes: 2026-04-09 (Conner): When this bridge gets built, it MUST filter on MealRecipe.diet_plan_id (NOT NULL FK to SystemTag, values are Omnivore / Plant-Based as of 2026-04-09). Do not use MealRecipe.dietary_tags String[] for the meat-vs-plant cut -- that field is for badges (Dairy Free, Gluten Friendly, etc.), not the classifier. Do not use MealRecipe.category either -- that is now purely meal-type (Entree/Breakfast/Snacks), no longer the meat/vegan split. linked_meal_id is unidirectional meat->plant only; use the Prisma variant_meals reverse relation to discover the omnivore dishes pointing at a given plant-based dish (supports N-to-1, e.g. one Vegan Alfredo paired with Shrimp/Chicken/Beef Alfredo). See culinary-ops PR #10 and ADR 2026-04-08-mandatory-diet-plan-on-dishes.md.
